tips.tip: Focus on one high-ROI skill this week. Mastering the basics deeply beats skimming many topics. Start with the skill that appears in the most job postings for your target role.
Build something visual this week. A portfolio component or a dashboard widget helps solidify React/CSS concepts and gives you something to show recruiters.
Set up a clean REST API with proper error handling and validation. Employers value well-structured backend code over feature quantity.
Start your first project now, even if it's small. A real project teaches 10x more than tutorials. Pick something that uses 2-3 of your gap skills.
Containerize one of your projects with Docker this week. This skill alone makes you 30% more marketable for mid-level roles.
You're one month in! Review your progress — which skills feel solid? Update your proficiency levels in the Skill Tracker.
Start practicing interview questions now, not later. Even 15 minutes daily builds confidence and reveals knowledge gaps you didn't know about.
Focus on building an end-to-end ML pipeline this week. Understanding the full workflow (data → model → deploy) matters more than algorithm theory.
Connect two skills together in a project. For example: React + Node.js API, or Python + Docker deployment. Interlinked skills are more valuable than isolated ones.
Update your resume with newly acquired skills and projects. Use the Resume Builder to check your ATS score against your target job.
Two-thirds done! Focus on depth over breadth now. If you've started 5 skills, make sure at least 3 are at Intermediate level.
Start polishing your portfolio projects. Add README files, deploy live demos, and clean up code. First impressions matter in code reviews.
Do at least 3 mock interviews this week. Focus on your weakest question types. Consistency beats cramming.
Refine your system design knowledge. Even for mid-level roles, understanding architecture trade-offs sets you apart.
Final week! Do a full mock interview, finalize your resume, and ensure your portfolio has 3+ deployed projects. You're ready to apply.
